user_query:
I'm putting together a weekly inspiration digest that tracks how a celebrity atheist talks about religion and related themes across my local sources. Please use only the provided files under input/ and produce three outputs:

1) A unified structured dataset at output/data/normalized_items.csv that merges items from all sources with one row per item. Include these columns: item_id, source_type (one of: quote, interview_answer, blog_post), date (YYYY-MM-DD), source_or_title, text (the item's full text), text_word_count, religion_count, faith_count, science_count, morality_count, freedom_count, reason_count. Treat each <div class="a"> answer inside input/interview.html as one item (source_type=interview_answer) and use the <time datetime> value for its date and the <h1> text for source_or_title. For quotes, use the id and source fields from input/quotes.csv; for blog posts, use id and title from input/blog_posts.jsonl.

2) Aggregated statistics at output/stats/aggregates.json containing:
   - overall_keyword_totals: an object with total case-insensitive whole-word occurrences of each keyword across all items for exactly these keywords: ["religion", "faith", "science", "morality", "freedom", "reason"].
   - items_per_source_type: counts of items by source_type.
   - average_word_count_per_source_type: average text_word_count by source_type.
   - items_per_year: counts of items grouped by year.
   - top_keywords_ranked: the six keywords sorted by descending total occurrences.

3) A concise report at output/report/inspiration_digest.md with four sections: Overview, Key stats, Notable quotes, Writing prompts. In Overview (2–3 sentences), summarize what the data suggests about the stance, explicitly naming the top two keywords from aggregates.json.top_keywords_ranked. In Key stats, list bullets that include overall totals for all six keywords and item counts by source_type and by year (numbers must match aggregates.json). In Notable quotes, include at least three exact quote lines drawn from input/quotes.csv whose text contains one of the top two keywords; for each, show its id and date. In Writing prompts, provide three constructive, bridge-building prompt ideas grounded in the observed top themes (avoid inflammatory language).

Counting rule for keywords and text_word_count: use case-insensitive whole-word matching where tokens are defined by splitting on non-letter characters; the six keywords to count are exactly: religion, faith, science, morality, freedom, reason. Do not use the live Internet; everything should be derived from the files provided.

Input paths: input/quotes.csv, input/interview.html, input/blog_posts.jsonl. Output paths: output/data/normalized_items.csv, output/stats/aggregates.json, output/report/inspiration_digest.md. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
